name: David's psalm of thanksgiving
notes: When David brought the ark to Zion he delivered into the hand of Asaph a single hymn of thanksgiving (1 Chronicles 16:8-36). Portions of that one psalm stand in the Psalter as the opening of Psalm 105 (the call to thanks and the remembrance of the covenant with Abraham), as nearly the whole of Psalm 96 (Sing unto the LORD all the earth, worship in the beauty of holiness, he cometh to judge the earth), and as the opening and closing verses of Psalm 106 (he is good, his mercy endureth for ever; Save us and gather us; Blessed be the LORD God of Israel). The wording is near-verbatim throughout. Where the Chronicles verse gathers into one line what the Psalter spreads across two, the single verse is linked to both. The small variations in wording (continually for evermore, seed of Israel for seed of Abraham, for ever and ever for everlasting to everlasting) are left as a matter for further study.
